text.title: AWS EC2 Provisioning Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `AWS EC2 Provisioning` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Launch templates MUST pin the AMI by ID (or Image Builder recipe version), not by `most_recent = true`.

rationale.source: AWS EC2 Launch Templates docs; CIS AWS Benchmark

rule.rationale: Auto-latest AMIs cause silent in-place upgrades during scaling; pinned IDs make rollouts reviewable.

rule.statement: IMDSv2 MUST be required (`http_tokens = required`, `http_put_response_hop_limit = 1`). IMDSv1 fallback is forbidden.

rationale.source: AWS Instance Metadata Service docs; SSRF postmortems

rule.rationale: IMDSv1 is the most-exploited metadata vector for SSRF → credential theft.

rule.statement: Operator access MUST go through SSM Session Manager + AWS-managed audit log. Inbound SSH (port 22) is forbidden in security groups for production.

rationale.source: AWS Systems Manager Session Manager docs

rule.rationale: SSH key sprawl + bastion-host management is the #1 IAM-bypass source; SSM logs every action.

rule.statement: Production EC2 MUST run in private subnets; egress via NAT Gateway. Public IPs require security-lead approval + WAF / SG justification.
rule.rationale: Public-subnet EC2 is the most common ransomware entry vector in AWS.

rule.statement: New EC2 launch templates MUST default to Graviton (ARM64) instance families; x86 requires ADR justification.
rule.rationale: Graviton is ~20% cheaper + comparable perf; defaulting to x86 is silent waste.

rule.statement: All EBS volumes MUST be encrypted with KMS; account-level `EnableEbsEncryptionByDefault` MUST be on.

rationale.source: AWS EBS Encryption docs; CIS AWS Benchmark

rule.rationale: Unencrypted EBS volumes are a SOC2 / HIPAA blocker + a leaked-snapshot surface.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
